perturbation.original_text:
10. TERM
10.1 Basic Term In relation to each Fund, this Agreement shall terminate on the earlier of (a) the expiration of the term of such Fund or (b) the date, if any, on which Oaktree US (or any affiliate it has substituted in its stead in accordance with such Fund's Fund Agreement) is removed as general partner of such Fund or (c) the Sub-Advisor ceasing to be authorised and regulated by the FCA.
10.2 Early Termination This Agreement may be terminated, either in respect of a Fund or in its entirety, by either Oaktree US or the Sub-Advisor for any reason upon 30 days' written notice to the other.
perturbation.changed_text:
10. TERM
10.1 Basic Term In relation to each Fund, this Agreement shall terminate on the earlier of (a) the expiration of the term of such Fund or (b) the date, if any, on which Oaktree US (or any affiliate it has substituted in its stead in accordance with such Fund's Fund Agreement) is removed as general partner of such Fund or (c) the Sub-Advisor ceasing to be authorised and regulated by the FCA.
10.2 Early Termination This Agreement may be terminated, either in respect of a Fund or in its entirety, by Oaktree US immediately, or by the Sub-Advisor for any reason upon 30 days' written notice to the other.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction in Clause 10.2. The original clause states that either party can terminate with 30 days' notice. The changed text allows Oaktree US to terminate immediately, while the Sub-Advisor still requires 30 days' notice. This contradicts the original intent of equal termination rights, causing uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10.2 Early Termination This Agreement may be terminated, either in respect of a Fund or in its entirety, by Oaktree US immediately, or by the Sub-Advisor for any reason upon 30 days' written notice to the other.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Clause 10.2, creating a clear contradiction. The original clause implies equal termination rights, but the changed text allows Oaktree US to terminate immediately while requiring the Sub-Advisor to provide 30 days' notice. This directly contradicts the initial implication of equal termination rights and introduces an inconsistency regarding termination procedures, making it a strong in-text contradiction. The Contradicted Text is the same as the changed text, further solidifying the in-text contradiction within Clause 10.2. This aligns with the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties'.

perturbation.original_text:
3. FEES
3.1 In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US will pay the Sub-Advisor such fees as may be agreed between the parties from time to time (the "Service Fee").
3.2 At Oaktree US' discretion, the Service Fee shall be reduced by any management fees received directly by the Sub-Advisor for investment management services provided to any party pursuant to this Agreement. The Service Fee shall also be reduced by any amounts earned on cash and cash-equivalents held by the Sub-Advisor pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text:
3. FEES
3.1 In consideration of the provision of services under this Agreement, Oaktree US will pay the Sub-Advisor such fees as may be agreed between the parties from time to time (the "Service Fee"). The Service Fee will be paid within 30 days of invoice.
3.2 At Oaktree US' discretion for the first year, and at Oaktree US's descretion but no more than 60 days for each following year, the Service Fee shall be reduced by any management fees received directly by the Sub-Advisor for investment management services provided to any party pursuant to this Agreement. The Service Fee shall also be reduced by any amounts earned on cash and cash-equivalents held by the Sub-Advisor pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: This perturbation creates an in-text contradiction by adding a payment deadline to 3.1 and altering the discretion around reduction of the fee in 3.2. By adding 'The Service Fee will be paid within 30 days of invoice.' to 3.1, we add a payment deadline. Then, by adding 'at Oaktree US's descretion but no more than 60 days for each following year' to 3.2, we are increasing the payment period from 30 to 60 days. This creates ambiguity and directly contradicts the payment terms, since it is unclear what payment period takes precedence, causing uncertainty and potential disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.2 At Oaktree US' discretion for the first year, and at Oaktree US's descretion but no more than 60 days for each following year, the Service Fee shall be reduced by any management fees received directly by the Sub-Advisor for investment management services provided to any party pursuant to this Agreement. The Service Fee shall also be reduced by any amounts earned on cash and cash-equivalents held by the Sub-Advisor pursuant to this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The addition of 'The Service Fee will be paid within 30 days of invoice.' in section 3.1 creates a direct contradiction with the revised section 3.2, which states that Oaktree US has discretion to reduce the Service Fee, but no more than 60 days for each following year. This implicitly extends the potential payment period up to 60 days through reduction, creating ambiguity and inconsistency. Therefore the Changed Text contradicts the Contradicted Text.
